metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5542
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1C: MINK and TNIK enriched in PSD biochemical fractions prepared from rat brain, as compared to S1, S2, P1, P2 fractions.

Figure 1D: In cultured hippocampal neurons at DIV23, a polyclonal antibody against MINK/TNIK showed localization to synaptic puncta (co-staining with Bassoon and Phalloidin, resolution does not allow for distinction between pre/post-synapse).

Figure 2C: RNAi knockdown of MINK in hippocampal cultures after 18 DIV significantly reduced dendritic arbor complexity relative to control neurons.

Figure 3: MINK and TNIK knockdown modulate spine density and surface expression of AMPA receptors.

Figure 4: MINK and TNIK localization in neurons is modulated by the activation state of Rap2.

Figure 6: MINK blocks Rap2-mediated reduction in surface GluR1 clusters.. Experimental description: Figure 1: "Western blot analysis of postnuclear supernatants isolated from adult rat tissues."

"MINK RNAi: 5'-GATCCCCGTACTCTCACCATCGCAATTTCAAGAGAATTGCGATGGTGAGAGTACTTTTTGGAAA-3' and 5'-AGCTTTTCCAAAAAGTACTCTCACCATCGCAATTCTCTTGAAATTGCGATGGTGAGAGTACGGG-3'. MINK RNAi sequences previously validated by (McCarty et al., 2005) were adapted for insertion into pSuper."

antibody:
MINK and MINK/TNIK antibodies from GeneTex
